name: Tafsir al-Mizan
author: Allamah Muhammad Husayn Tabatabai
text:
# Ayahs

```arabic
وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَآ إِلَىٰ ثَمُودَ أَخَاهُمْ صَـٰلِحًا أَنِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ فَإِذَا هُمْ فَرِيقَانِ يَخْتَصِمُونَ (٤٥)
```

```english
Certainly We sent to Thamud Salih, their brother, [with the summons:] ‘Worship Allah!’ But thereat they became two groups contending with each other. (45)
```

```arabic
قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ لِمَ تَسْتَعْجِلُونَ بِٱلسَّيِّئَةِ قَبْلَ ٱلْحَسَنَةِ ۖ لَوْلَا تَسْتَغْفِرُونَ ٱللَّهَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُرْحَمُونَ (٤٦)
```

```english
He said, ‘O My people! Why do you press for evil sooner than for good? Why do you not plead to Allah for forgiveness so that you may receive His mercy?’ (46)
```

```arabic
قَالُوا۟ ٱطَّيَّرْنَا بِكَ وَبِمَن مَّعَكَ ۚ قَالَ طَـٰٓئِرُكُمْ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ ۖ بَلْ أَنتُمْ قَوْمٌ تُفْتَنُونَ (٤٧)
```

```english
They said, ‘We take you and those who are with you for a bad omen.’ He said, ‘Your bad omens are from Allah. Indeed, you are a people being tested.’ (47)
```

```arabic
وَكَانَ فِى ٱلْمَدِينَةِ تِسْعَةُ رَهْطٍ يُفْسِدُونَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَلَا يُصْلِحُونَ (٤٨)
```

```english
There were nine persons in the city who caused corruption in the land and did not set things right. (48)
```

```arabic
قَالُوا۟ تَقَاسَمُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ لَنُبَيِّتَنَّهُۥ وَأَهْلَهُۥ ثُمَّ لَنَقُولَنَّ لِوَلِيِّهِۦ مَا شَهِدْنَا مَهْلِكَ أَهْلِهِۦ وَإِنَّا لَصَـٰدِقُونَ (٤٩)
```

```english
They said, ‘Swear by Allah that we will attack him and his family by night. Then we will tell his heir that we were not present at the murder of his family and that we speak the truth.’ (49)
```

```arabic
وَمَكَرُوا۟ مَكْرًا وَمَكَرْنَا مَكْرًا وَهُمْ لَا يَشْعُرُونَ (٥٠)
```

```english
They devised a plot, and We [too] devised a plan, but they were not aware. (50)
```

```arabic
فَٱنظُرْ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ مَكْرِهِمْ أَنَّا دَمَّرْنَـٰهُمْ وَقَوْمَهُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ (٥١)
```

```english
So observe how was the outcome of their plotting, as We destroyed them and all their people. (51)
```

```arabic
فَتِلْكَ بُيُوتُهُمْ خَاوِيَةًۢ بِمَا ظَلَمُوٓا۟ ۗ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَةً لِّقَوْمٍ يَعْلَمُونَ (٥٢)
```

```english
So there lay their houses, fallen in ruin because of their wrongdoing. There is indeed a sign in that for a people who have knowledge. (52)
```

```arabic
وَأَنجَيْنَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَكَانُوا۟ يَتَّقُونَ (٥٣)
```

```english
And We delivered those who had faith and were Godwary. (53)
```

## (Commentary)

This is a summary of the story of the Prophet Salih (عليه السلام) and his people. The aspect of warning (الإنذار) in these verses prevails over the aspect of glad tidings (التبشير), as previously indicated.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنا إِلى ثَمُودَ أَخاهُمْ صالِحاً** — until His saying — **يَخْتَصِمُونَ** (**Certainly We sent to Thamud their brother Salih, [saying], ‘Worship Allah!’ Behold, they became two factions at odds with each other.** 27:45). Contention (الاختصام) and disputation (التخاصم) refer to quarreling (التنازع). The dual noun "two factions" (فَرِيقانِ) is described with a plural verb in His saying "at odds with each other" (يَخْتَصِمُونَ) because the intended meaning of the two factions is the entire community (الأمة). The word "Behold" (فَإِذا) indicates a sudden event (فجائية).

The meaning is: I swear, We certainly sent to the people of Thamud their brother and kinsman Salih. It was hoped that they would unite upon faith (الإيمان), but suddenly they split into two factions—believers and disbelievers—contending and quarreling over the truth, each saying, "The truth is with me." Perhaps their contention refers to what Allah narrated about them elsewhere in His saying: **قالَ الْمَلَأُ الَّذِينَ اسْتَكْبَرُوا مِنْ قَوْمِهِ لِلَّذِينَ اسْتُضْعِفُوا لِمَنْ آمَنَ مِنْهُمْ أَتَعْلَمُونَ أَنَّ صالِحاً مُرْسَلٌ مِنْ رَبِّهِ قالُوا إِنَّا بِما أُرْسِلَ بِهِ مُؤْمِنُونَ قالَ الَّذِينَ اسْتَكْبَرُوا إِنَّا بِالَّذِي آمَنْتُمْ بِهِ كافِرُونَ** (**The elite of his people who were arrogant said to those who were oppressed—to those among them who had faith—‘Do you know that Salih has been sent by his Lord?’ They said, ‘We indeed believe in what he has been sent with.’ Those who were arrogant said, ‘We indeed disbelieve in what you believe in.’** 7:75-76).

From this, it becomes clear that one of the two factions was a group of the oppressed (المستضعفين) who believed in him, while the other consisted of the arrogant (المستكبرون) and the remaining oppressed who followed their leaders.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **قالَ يا قَوْمِ لِمَ تَسْتَعْجِلُونَ بِالسَّيِّئَةِ قَبْلَ الْحَسَنَةِ** (**He said, ‘O my people! Why do you seek to hasten the evil before the good?** 27:46) etc. Hastening the evil (السيئة) before the good (الحسنة) means rushing to ask for punishment (العذاب) before seeking the mercy (الرحمة) that is brought about by faith and seeking forgiveness (الاستغفار).

Through this, it appears that Salih (عليه السلام) only rebuked them with these words after they had hamstrung the she-camel and said to him: **يا صالِحُ ائْتِنا بِما تَعِدُنا إِنْ كُنْتَ مِنَ الْمُرْسَلِينَ** (**‘O Salih, bring us what you threaten us with, if you are one of the apostles.’** 7:77). Thus, his saying: **لَوْ لا تَسْتَغْفِرُونَ اللهَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُرْحَمُونَ** (**Why do you not plead to Allah for forgiveness, so that you may receive His mercy?’** 27:46) is an exhortation (تحضيض) toward faith and repentance (التوبة), so that Allah might have mercy on them and remove the punishment He had promised them—a promise that was not a lie.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **قالُوا اطَّيَّرْنا بِكَ وَبِمَنْ مَعَكَ قالَ طائِرُكُمْ عِنْدَ اللهِ** (**They said, ‘We take you and those who are with you for an ill omen.’ He said, ‘Your omen is with Allah.** 27:47) etc. Taking an ill omen (التطير) is to see something as a bad portent (التشؤم). They used to frequently take omens from birds (الطير), which is why the act of seeing a bad portent was called *tataiyur* (التطير). A person's portion of evil was referred to as a "bird" (طائراً), as has been said.

So their statement addressing Salih: **اطَّيَّرْنا بِكَ وَبِمَنْ مَعَكَ** ("We take you and those who are with you for an ill omen") means: We see a bad portent in you and those who have believed in you and stayed with you, because your undertaking of the mission and their faith in you coincided with the trials and calamities we have been afflicted with; therefore, we will not believe in you.

His statement addressing the people: **طائِرُكُمْ عِنْدَ اللهِ** ("Your omen is with Allah") means: Your portion of evil—which is the punishment necessitated by your deeds—is with Allah, the Glorified.

For this reason, He turned away from the statement "Your omen is with Allah" by saying: **بَلْ أَنْتُمْ قَوْمٌ تُفْتَنُونَ** (**Rather you are a people being tested.’** 27:47), meaning you are being tried (تختبرون) with good and evil so that the believer among you may be distinguished from the disbeliever, and the obedient from the disobedient.

The meaning of the verse is: The people said, "We see a bad omen in you, O Salih, and in those with you, so we will neither believe nor seek forgiveness." Salih replied, "Your 'bird'—which contains your portion of evil—is with Allah; it is the record of your deeds. Neither I nor those with me have any effect upon you such that we would drive these trials toward you. Rather, you are a people being tested and tried through these matters so that the believer may be distinguished from the disbeliever, and the obedient from the disobedient."

It has also been said that the "bird" (الطائر) is the cause from which a person receives whatever good or evil befalls him. Just as they took ill omens from birds, they also took good omens from them. To them, the "bird" was that which confronts a person with good or evil, as in His saying, the Exalted: **وَكُلَّ إِنسانٍ أَلْزَمْناهُ طائِرَهُ فِي عُنُقِهِ وَنُخْرِجُ لَهُ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ كِتاباً** (**We have fastened every man’s omen to his neck, and on the Day of Resurrection We shall bring out for him a book** 17:13). Since whatever good or evil confronts a person is by the decree (قضاء) of Allah, the Glorified, written in a book, the "bird" is the Preserved Book (الكتاب) in which what is destined for man is ordained.

However, the apparent meaning of the end of the verse in Surah al-Isra is that the "bird" refers to the record of deeds (كتاب الأعمال) rather than the book of decree (كتاب القضاء), as indicated by His saying: **اقْرَأْ كِتابَكَ كَفى بِنَفْسِكَ الْيَوْمَ عَلَيْكَ حَسِيباً** (**‘Read your book! Today your own soul suffices as your reckoner.’** 17:14).

It has also been said that the meaning of **بَلْ أَنْتُمْ قَوْمٌ تُفْتَنُونَ** is "rather you are a people being punished," but what we mentioned first is more appropriate.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **وَكانَ فِي الْمَدِينَةِ تِسْعَةُ رَهْطٍ** (**There were nine cliques in the city** 27:48) etc. Al-Raghib said: A clique (الرهط) is a group (العصابة) of fewer than ten, and it is said up to forty. It has also been said that the difference between *raht* (الرهط) and *nafar* (النفر) is that *raht* is from three or seven up to ten, while *nafar* is from three to nine.

It is said that *raht* here refers to individuals, which is why it serves as the specification (تمييز) for the number nine, as it carries the meaning of a plural; thus, those who took the oath were nine men.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **قالُوا تَقاسَمُوا بِاللهِ لَنُبَيِّتَنَّهُ وَأَهْلَهُ ثُمَّ لَنَقُولَنَّ لِوَلِيِّهِ ما شَهِدْنا مَهْلِكَ أَهْلِهِ وَإِنَّا لَصادِقُونَ** (**They said, ‘Swear to one another by Allah: We will surely attack him and his family by night, then we will tell his blood-wit guardian, “We were not present at the destruction of his family, and we are indeed truthful.” ’** 27:49). Taking an oath among one another (التقاسم) is to participate in a collective swearing. Attacking by night (التبييت) is to intend harm during the night. A man's family (أهل) are those joined with him by house, lineage, or religion. Perhaps the intended meaning of his family here is his wife and children, given the context of the subsequent phrase: "then we will tell his blood-wit guardian (لِوَلِيِّهِ) 'We were not present...'" His saying: "and we are indeed truthful" (وَإِنَّا لَصادِقُونَ) is coordinated with "We were not present" (ما شَهِدْنا), thus forming part of what was said.

The meaning is: The corrupt clique said, having sworn to one another by Allah: "We will surely kill him and his family by night, then we will say to his guardian—when he follows up and seeks vengeance—'We did not witness the destruction of his family, and we are indeed truthful in this statement.'" Denying the witnessing of the destruction of his family is a denial of witnessing his own destruction by implication or by greater reason (الأولوية), as has been said.

It has also been suggested that His saying: "and we are indeed truthful" (وَإِنَّا لَصادِقُونَ) is a circumstantial clause (حال) referring to the subject of "we will say," meaning: We will say such to his guardian while we are truthful in this statement, because we witnessed the destruction of both him and his family together, not the destruction of his family alone.

The forced nature (التكلف) of this interpretation is not hidden, and it has been justified in other ways even more forced than this; there is no necessity to consider it a circumstantial clause in the first place.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **وَمَكَرُوا مَكْراً وَمَكَرْنا مَكْراً وَهُمْ لا يَشْعُرُونَ** (**They devised a stratagem, and We [too] devised a stratagem, while they were not aware.** 27:50). As for their stratagem (مكرهم), it was the conspiracy to attack him and his family by night and the taking of the oath, as evidenced by the preceding context. As for the stratagem of Allah (مكره تعالى), it was His decreeing the destruction of them all, as evidenced by the subsequent context.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **فَانْظُرْ كَيْفَ كانَ عاقِبَةُ مَكْرِهِمْ أَنَّا دَمَّرْناهُمْ وَقَوْمَهُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ** (**So observe how was the outcome of their stratagem: that We destroyed them and their people, all together.** 27:51). Destruction (التدمير) is annihilation (الإهلاك). The plural pronouns refer to the clique. The fact that the outcome of their stratagem was the destruction of them and their people is because their stratagem invited the Divine stratagem by way of retribution (المجازاة), which necessitated the destruction of them and their people.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **فَتِلْكَ بُيُوتُهُمْ خاوِيَةً بِما ظَلَمُوا** (**So there are their houses, empty because of their wrongdoing.** 27:52) etc. Empty (الخاوية) means vacant, derived from *khawa’* (الخواء) meaning emptiness (الخلاء). The rest is clear.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **وَأَنْجَيْنَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَكانُوا يَتَّقُونَ** (**And We delivered those who had faith and were God-conscious.** 27:53). In this is a glad tiding for the believers of deliverance (الإنجاء). He followed it with His saying: "and were God-conscious" (وَكانُوا يَتَّقُونَ) because God-consciousness (التقوى) — *the internal shield of piety and protection against sin* — is like a shield for faith. Indeed, the Exalted has said: **وَالْعاقِبَةُ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ** (**and the outcome will be in favor of the God-conscious.** 7:128) and He said: **وَالْعاقِبَةُ لِلتَّقْوى** (**and the [good] outcome is for God-consciousness.** 20:132).
